summary.intent: Address two new Codex review threads on PR #679 in fogodev/ars-ui, fixing roving-tabindex invariants in MenuBar and context menu reopen state reset, then get CI green and the PR ready to merge.
summary.outcome: Both review threads were resolved with a new commit fixing MenuBar tab-entry preservation after blur, context menu transient state reset on reopen, and a disabled-component guard in the Blur transition; all unit tests, proptests, clippy, fmt, and spec validation passed, and the fix was pushed.

repo: MenuBar uses a roving-tabindex pattern: exactly one trigger must remain the tab entry point at all times, including after blur and when menus are open.
MenuBar's Blur transition must check props.disabled before restoring the focus entry point, because Blur can fire even when the component is disabled.
ContextMenu's open transition must reset transient submenu and typeahead state so reopening does not inherit stale UI state from a previous open.
The repo uses cargo xtask spec validate to enforce consistency between spec markdown files and component implementations.
Proptest state machines (cargo test --test proptest_state_machines --ignored menu_bar) are used to catch edge-case transition invariants that unit tests miss.
MenuBar direction routing for arrow keys must resolve Direction::Auto from the stored locale before applying RTL/LTR arrow-key dispatch.
Codex reviews are triggered via a PR comment '@codex review' and tracked via GitHub reactions (eyes = in progress, removal = done) and review threads.

code.finding: Roving tabindex logic and menu trigger attribute emission live here; the tab-entry trigger must be preserved after blur and must account for disabled state.
Blur transition handler — requires disabled guard before restoring focus entry point.
ContextOpen transition — must clear pointer position, submenu, and typeahead state before setting new open state to avoid stale reopen behavior.

workflow: The fix cycle follows a strict test-first loop: add failing assertions, verify they fail, apply production fix, verify they pass, then run the full suite before committing.
Codex review progress is polled via a compound shell command checking GitHub reactions, open review threads (GraphQL), and CI status checks in a single call with 120s sleeps.
All four checks (clippy, fmt --check, diff --check, spec validate) are bundled into a single cargo pipeline command to catch regressions in one pass before committing.
PR replies and thread resolution are done atomically in a single compound gh command to keep the review state consistent.

friction: Codex fourth review pass took significantly longer than expected, with multiple 120s polling cycles showing 'eyes' reaction before producing threads — suspected external stall.
The proptest caught a disabled-component edge case (Blur firing while disabled) that unit tests did not cover, requiring an additional fix-and-rerun cycle after the main fix was believed complete.
The roving-tabindex fix required understanding the distinction between attrs-level and state-machine-level invariants — initial read of the thread suggested only attrs changes were needed.
open_items: No further open items surfaced in this session; all threads were resolved and CI was restarted on the final commit. Future reviewers should verify the disabled-Blur guard holds under any new disabled-state transitions added to MenuBar.
